name: Dialogues with Books: Monthly Meetup
description: Join Bangalore`s Iconic Book Community - Monthly DWB Meetup! 📚Join Dialogues with Books (DWB) for our monthly gathering where fellow bibliophiles come together to celebrate the joy of reading!What We`ll Do -Come with a book you`ve been enjoying lately - any genre welcome! We`ll all share what we`re reading, why it resonates with us, and dive into lively discussions about the stories and ideas that captivate us. Feel free to read favourite passages aloud and discover your next great read through fellow bibliophiles` recommendations.Expect an evening of engaging book conversations over warm beverages, light snacks, and great company!About DWB - Dialogues with Books is one of Bangalore`s oldest book communities, creating a vibrant, inclusive space where literature lovers share their passion and form lasting friendships through books. Whether you`re a seasoned reader or looking to reconnect with reading, you`ll find a warm welcome here.DWB is Perfect For: Book enthusiasts, readers wanting to discover new genres, and anyone seeking thoughtful literary conversations in Bangalore`s vibrant reading community.Location: Dialogues KoramangalaMeet Your Host: Bharat - Bharat is an avid reader, writer, and passionate bibliophile with eclectic literary tastes spanning classics, fiction, philosophy, poetry, and self-improvement. His favorite authors include Hemingway, Murakami, Mishima, P.G. Wodehouse, and Edgar Allan Poe. His infectious enthusiasm for books and skill at facilitating discussions makes every meetup both stimulating and enjoyable.

name: Ramzan iftar walk in Fraser town.
description: Step into the warm, lived-in lanes of Fraser Town and join us for an intimate Iftar walk that feels less like a restaurant spread and more like a beautifully kept family secret. This is a carefully curated feast of 12–14 soulful courses, celebrating traditional Muslim kitchens from across the country — each dish shaped by memory, region and ritual.The first notes are gentle and comforting: a bowl of soul-warming harira, crisp golden samosas and a deeply aromatic, slow-cooked haleem that clings lovingly to the spoon. Soon, the table fills with indulgence — delicate brain puffs, juicy shammis, sinful koftas and pillowy, freshly baked naan, served alongside richly spiced chicken and mutton gravies that demand to be torn into and savoured by hand.The journey continues with fragrant biryani, indulgent shawarma rolls, tender quail and smoky sheekh kebabs — each bite revealing a different accent of the subcontinent’s Muslim culinary heritage.What truly elevates this Iftar is that several dishes are prepared the way they are meant to be — home-made, family-style recipes that rarely leave private kitchens. The kind of food you do not find on printed menus, only on trusted tables.Just when you think you can manage no more, a soothing cup of Sulaimani chai arrives — light, fragrant and quietly restorative — inviting you to slow down and savour the moment. To refresh the palate, we serve a cooling Rooh Afza with a bright splash of lemon and delicate chia seeds.The finale is unapologetically indulgent: soft egg pudding, jewel-toned khubani ka meetha, the ever-beloved double ka meetha — and, not to be missed, an irresistibly overloaded khoya naan.Between mouthfuls and memories, we gently weave in stories of Fraser Town’s vibrant past, along with the traditions of Ramzan and the deeper meaning of fasting in Islam — adding a warm cultural layer to an already rich culinary journey.Note: All dishes mentioned are subject to availability.Duration: Approximately 2.5 hours.

name: Coffee and Conversations
description: What about some Filter Coffee and Unfiltered conversations on a fine Sunday morning?Listeners Circle is an initiative to facilitate listening without judgment, hence giving a safe space for anyone to speak their heart out without the fear of judgment.We at Listeners Circle are committed to building a community where there are no unsaid words, held back emotions and buried feelings because of the fear of being judged.After 50+ Editions of Tell your Story, Listeners Circle relaunching a lighter format - Coffee and Conversations!Coffee lightens us up. Coffee gives us clarity. Coffee refreshes up. Sip a coffee and speak your heart out without fear of judgment and listen without judging! Coffee is on us and the onus of conversation without judgment is on you.Every word matters. Every emotion matters. Every feeling matters.Think about how it would feel to take an hour out of your fast-paced life and share your experiences and thoughts to people who listen to you without judging over a cup of Coffee? Also, listen to others without judging.Listeners` circle Coffee and Conversations -This is an event to make that happen.Speak your mind without being judged. Speak your mind without any boundaries. No photography and videography without consent. Your stories stay within the four walls of the room and the hearts.Would you like to just listen? We have a place for you too.Book your passes in advance to avoid the last-minute sell outs.Listeners Circle is an initiative to facilitate listening without judgment, hence giving a safe space for anyone to speak their heart out without the fear of judgment. We have done 50+ sessions over the last year, enabling people to voice their stories. Coffee & Conversations is our new format based on popular demand.

name: Atta Galatta presents “Support Group”
description: The scars left behind by the terrible experience of sexual abuse during childhood is among the most difficult to heal, which impacts everything from how one perceives oneself, perception of relationships and other people in general. This special edition of support group aims at facilitating a discussion around the challenges of surviving sexual abuse. The gentle discussion will be centered around:How the abuse has impacted one’s sense of selfHow relationship with parents have been impactedStruggles with experiences in the bodyDifficulties in romantic relationshipFeeling unsafe and difficulties in establishing trusting relationshipsHow to escape the cycle of repeated feelings pertaining to the experienceHow to rempower the selfIf you wish to not refer to your experience and simply be a part of the discussion, you too are welcomeAge: 18 and above

description: Got a single friend who’s a total catch? 👀This is your chance to be the ultimate wing-person. All revealed at the end!🍸 Single-Mingle Party: Stay back for drinks and conversations that dating apps can’t create.Whether you’re pitching a friend, cheering them on, or just there to meet new people — this is your night to laugh, mingle, and maybe leave with a story worth telling.

name: Dinner with strangers by StepOut (Bengaluru)
description: Serendipity on a TableStepOut Dinners bring strangers together at some of the city’s best restaurants for an evening that feels easy, warm, and unexpectedly meaningful.You are seated at a small, thoughtfully curated table with people you would likely never meet otherwise. Different backgrounds, different stories, one shared meal. The crowd is balanced, open minded, and there by choice, not chance. No loud networking. No awkward icebreakers. Just good food, unforced conversations, and the kind of listening that rarely happens in daily life.The restaurants are carefully chosen. Comfortable, well loved spaces where you can settle in and talk without rushing. The focus is not on the menu alone, but on the atmosphere that allows connection to unfold naturally.Some nights are light and playful. Some go deeper than expected. Some surprise you quietly. That is the point.StepOut Dinners are not about dating or outcomes. They are about discovery. Showing up. Sitting down. Letting the table do its quiet work.Sometimes, all it takes is one seat at the right table for life to feel a little more open. For more information, visit www.stepout.world

name: Turtle Forum 2026
description: Turtle Forum is 3-day residential bootcamp (23–25 April, Bengaluru) designed for development practitioners working on social media, communications, and fundraising. The forum is focused on helping nonprofits rethink storytelling for new-age audiences, build participatory digital campaigns, and strengthen their online presence beyond program updates and impact numbers.The bootcamp brings together practitioners from journalism, media, design, and democracy to work on:writing and storytelling for social mediaparticipatory campaign designbuilding online communities that support your causeusing humour (responsibly) in nonprofit communicationWe feel this could be especially useful for someone who works closely on communications or fundraising, and is thinking about how to connect with younger, more engaged digital audiences.

name: Come Sit With Us: A Connection Circle
description: Come sit with usBecause stories of separation, healing & becoming are not heard often enough.Because we need more spaces, intimate gatherings, where real personal journeys are shared.What to Expect• Curated personal story readouts (anonymous or named)• Reflections and insights from a therapist• A space for the conversations that the stories brought on - unstructured, unfiltered - a space to connect, talk, or simply beThis is not a panel or a lecture but a space to listen, relate, and connect—at your own pace.Why This SpaceBecause separation and relationship transitions are rarely talked about honestly and without judgment.Because stories reduce isolation.Because healing doesn’t always need fixing—sometimes it needs witnessing.Do You Want to Share Your Story?You’re invited to submit a personal story around marriage, separation, divorce, or emotional transition here.Some selected stories will be read aloud during the gathering.With consent, all submitted stories will also be shared on our Substack (anonymously, if requested).Whether you’re navigating this personally, witnessing it in close circles, or engaging with it academically, we're pulling up a chair for you.

name: That’s What She Said: Playback Theatre Performance
description: On the occasion of Women’s Day, we ask a simple question:Why should women be celebrated only on one day?Women are powerful, complex, and extraordinary every single day—yet too often treated like second-hand citizens the rest of the year. This performance begins right there, challenging the idea of limiting celebration to a date on the calendar.Instead, we open a space for real stories.Stories of being a woman.Stories of loving, learning from, and living alongside women.Stories of mothers, teachers, mothers-in-law, partners, sisters, daughters, friends, bosses, colleagues, daughters-in-law—and everything in between.Stories of how men see the women in their lives.Stories of embracing the feminine within.What to ExpectThis unique Playback Theatre experience invites the audience to share personal moments and lived experiences. In real time, our performers will transform these stories into spontaneous theatre using movement, music, and dialogue—honest, unscripted, and deeply human.Threads and Tales is an Applied theater initiative by Lahe Lahe Caravan⏱️When: 8th March | 5:30 – 7:00 PM📍 Where: Atta Galata, Indiranagar🎟 Tickets: 300 rs per personAge: 18 and above

name: Classical Guitar Concert
description: Guitar Sabha is an assembly of sound, story, and tradition, where the classical guitar becomes a meeting point of cultures across centuries.This concert travels from the luminous architecture of Bach’s Suite in E major (BWV 1006a) to the passionate colours of Spain in Albéniz’s Sevilla and Asturias, and the iconic tremolo of Tárrega’s Recuerdos de la Alhambra. Rossini’s operatic brilliance shines through Rossiniana, while contemporary works like Benga Beat and Shamal bring modern rhythm and atmosphere to the programme.Together, these works reveal the versatility of the guitar, exploring traditional themes that expand into new sonic worlds.For more details about the artist please visit: sidharthsarangi.in

name: Gliding Emotions Tour | Tamil Jazz Collective Live
description: Tamil Jazz CollectiveLive at The Humming Tree, BangaloreMarch 19th, 2026Tamil Jazz Collective hits the road for a special 5-city, 5-day tour across India in collaboration with Greater Than.Blending the lyricism of Tamil with the harmony and improvisational spirit of jazz, Tamil Jazz Collective reimagines what jazz can sound like when it speaks in a new tongue. What began as an exploration of translating jazz standards into Tamil has now evolved into a distinctive sonic identity, emotive, intimate, and deeply expressive.The Gliding Emotions Tour celebrates their latest single, a piece that floats between vulnerability and groove, space and swing, carried by rich vocal textures, dynamic improvisation, and a band that listens as deeply as it plays.Special Opening FeatureInstead of a traditional opening act, the evening will begin with a specially curated literary segment in collaboration with The Humming Tree:A 15-minute live reading of Tamil Pulp FictionA 15-minute conversation with Blaft PublicationsA celebration of Tamil storytelling before we glide into musical storytelling.Important Venue InformationPlease note:A mandatory ₹500 cover charge will be collected at the venue entrance.This amount is fully redeemable on food and beverages inside the venue.The cover charge is separate from your concert ticket and is a venue policy applicable to all guests.We recommend arriving early to enjoy the full evening experience.Tour ScheduleMarch 18 – Bonobo, MumbaiMarch 19 – The Humming Tree, BangaloreMarch 20 – Bonobo, GoaMarch 21 – Skinny Mo’s, KolkataMarch 22 – The Piano Man Jazz Club, Delhi

name: Ode to Broken Things: Book Launch
description: Join us to celebrate Shruti Kulkarni’s debut poetry collection and reading from her book Ode to Broken Things.The poet will be in conversation with Vinutha Mallya.Ode to Broken Things is a reflection on what it means to be human, to be a living repository of timestamps of our individual and collective struggles and celebrations and of rebellions and failures. These poems are a quiet observation of our responses to the passing of these events and of our efforts to understand this world that offers experiences of duality — of wars and peacetime, abundance and poverty, of love and hate. Ode to Broken Things is also a prayer and an earnest dream for a peaceful world for everyone, where all hearts are warm and borders are always open.In Ode to Broken Things, we are taken to the heart of matter — to observe the atomic, the molecular, the granular parts of our inner and outer worlds that we miss amid the unsettling busyness. Ode to Broken Things invites us to feel, see, think and wonder about all that surrounds us. Raw and uncut, these poetic expressions draw us towards hope. – Vinutha Mallya [Journalist, editor and lecturer] Age: 14 and above

name: In Person Family Constellation
description: Family Constellation CircleSome patterns are inherited. Some stories are waiting to be seen.There are struggles that did not begin with you.They move quietly through family lines, shaping emotions, relationships, fears, and choices in ways that are often invisible.Family Constellation is a gentle yet profound group process that brings hidden family dynamics into awareness. When what was unseen becomes visible, something begins to settle. Not by force. Not by analysis. But through recognition.This is not talk therapy. This is experiential.In a respectful, grounded circle, we explore the deeper layers beneath repeating patterns, unexplained heaviness, relational blocks, guilt, grief, anxiety, or feeling “stuck” despite doing inner work.Many participants share that what shifts is subtle yet unmistakable:A calmer nervous systemA sense of clarity that was missingEmotional release without reliving traumaA quiet understanding of “this makes sense now” Even those who attend as representatives or observers often experience personal insight and movement within their own lives.You may feel called to attend if you: Keep repeating similar relationship or emotional patterns.Carry guilt, fear, or heaviness without a clear reason.Feel blocked despite personal growth efforts.Sense that something older than you is asking to be acknowledged.﻿What to Expect: A safe, confidential group setting Guided facilitation with care and structureDeep yet gentle explorationNo prior knowledge or preparation requiredYou do not need to understand the process for it to workSometimes presence is enough.Link for pre-session questionaire for client

name: udal / osai
description: About the event:A work-in-progress sharing of a collaborative performance project created by Abhinaya- a dance practitioner from New Delhi, and Sasikumar- a Kattaikkuttu artist and Mukavinai player from Kanchipuram. Mukavinai is a wind instrument primarily used in Kattaikkuttu, a traditional form of musical theatre practiced in Tamil speaking rural regions. It is used as a mood and ambience enhancer with its melodic interventions, and as a supporting instrument for the performers and chorus in its present context. Abhinaya and Sasikumar have been exploring different genres of melody and movement, to see the diverse ways in which the body can meet the sound, and all the different spaces (physically and aurally) the mukavinai and the body can occupy together.The first phase of this project was supported by the Department of Performing Arts at Ashoka, and was developed over a short period of time at Kattaikkuttu Sangam in Kanchipuram, Tamil Nadu.Tickets Mandatory for all.*

name: Flash Jam x Pulp at Coro
description: Join us on Saturday, March 7 at 11:00 AM for a super-fun Flash Jam led by Danish Abdi, where we’ll sing “Golden” from K-Pop Demon Hunters together! If you are wondering how this song was so sticky for kids and adults alike, we will also dive into PULP’s music book - Hook That Earworm. Take a copy of the book home after the jam!Age no bar, everyone is welcome to sing along! This is a special collab event between Coro × PULP × Danish Abdi/Flash Jam.About Nanditha GopalI’m a corporate lawyer (by day) and the mother of a curious son who asks more questions than I can ever answer. PULP is born out of 90s nostalgia, a love of bold design, the memory of spending a whole day diving into a mag, and the belief that wonder is worth holding on to. PULP is a collectible print series of 5 books for curious children (and their parents!) that dives deep into one fascinating theme at a time. From outer space to sports to music to nature – we aim to peel back the layers and go right to the heart of a concept to understand more.About Flash Jam / Danish AbdiFlash Jam started with a simple question: What if strangers could connect through singing — without pressure or performance? Today, Flash Jam is a growing community of people who show up for music, connection, and joy. If you love music but never sing, sing secretly, or haven't sung in years — Flash Jam is for you.

name: ALT EFF Film Club - Bangalore | 17th April
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.APRIL CURATION: "The Climate Baby Dilemma"Synopsis: "The Climate Baby Dilemma" by filmmaker Victoria Lean follows science writer Britt Wray as she explores the decision to have children amid climate change. Over three years, she engages with activists and parents, reflecting generational concerns. Featuring Sarain Fox and Severn Suzuki, the film offers insights on transforming climate anxiety into action.PROGRAMME17th April 2026, Friday7:00 - 9:00 PM: The Climate Baby Dilemma | Victoria Lean | Canada | 44 mins | English | 2022All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening). CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

name: Beethoven: The Complete Cello Sonatas
description: Presented by the South Asian Symphony FoundationRaman Ramakrishnan, cello | Stephen Prutsman, pianoBeethoven’s cello sonatas are a musical life story in miniature. Written over more than 30 years, they span all three of the composer’s creative periods—from the elegance of his early works through the bold confidence of his middle period to the searching, intimate language of his late style. Along the way, Beethoven reshapes the cello sonata into a true dialogue, with the cello and piano as equal partners in conversation. By turns energetic, lyrical, playful and deeply reflective, these sonatas offer listeners a rare chance to experience Beethoven’s imagination not in fragments but as a compelling journey.Cellist Raman Ramakrishnan is an internationally respected chamber musician, best known as a founding member of the Horszowski Trio and the Daedalus Quartet, with whom he has toured widely and recorded extensively. He is an artiste member of the Boston Chamber Music Society, serves on the faculty of the Bard College Conservatory of Music, New York, and has performed at major venues and festivals across the US, Europe and Asia.Pianist Stephen Prutsman has been described as one of the most innovative musicians of his time. A winner at the Tchaikovsky and Queen Elisabeth Piano Competitions in the early ’90s and moving seamlessly from classical to jazz to world music styles as a pianist and composer, Prutsman continues to explore and seek common ground and relationships in the music of all cultures and languages.About the South Asian Symphony Foundation The South Asian Symphony Foundation (SASF) was founded by Ambassador Nirupama Menon Rao with the aim of promoting greater cultural integration for the cause of peace in our region of South Asia, through the medium of music and the creation of a South Asian Symphony Orchestra. The inspiration comes from Ambassador Rao’s years in diplomacy and what she saw as a felt need for providing a platform to promote more dialogue, cultural synergy, and friendly understanding among the youth of the eight countries in South Asia, including India.

name: Mr. Ago and the Plastic People
description: An evening of immersive art-rock arrives at Ram’s Musique Experience.Mr. Ago and the Plastic People is the art-rock project of Bangalore/Kochi-based singer-songwriter Abhishek Gopurathinkal. His sound moves fluidly across genres — weaving symphonic textures with elements of blues, jazz, indie, and alternative rock. Influenced by artists such as Radiohead, Pink Floyd, Nina Simone, Arctic Monkeys, and Jimi Hendrix, the project inhabits a sonic space that feels both expansive and intimate.With the release of his debut single Perfect Lies in 2017 and the self-titled triptych EP Digital Muse in 2019, Mr. Ago established a distinct identity — immersive, contemplative, and quietly unsettling. The music does not rush toward resolution; it lingers in tension, atmosphere, and emotional complexity.If his sound had a colour, it would be bluish grey — edged with melancholy, threaded with broodiness, and illuminated by sudden surges of intensity.This performance at Ram’s Musique Experience brings that layered soundscape into an intimate listening room — where distortion, silence, and vulnerability can coexist without distraction.

name: Journey of a Song- 150 years of Vande Mataram
description: A poetry. A song. A novel. And their tumultuous journey of 150 years.Vande Mataram, which was drawn into the vortex of history. Vande Mataram, which moulded history in its own term.Stories and anecdotes which have stalked the song. Personalities who have drifted into that journey. Facts, myths and misgivings – all of which have painted the canvas and lent to the enigma of a call: Vande Mataram.Nivedita and Ashis take the audience through this fascinating journey, and sometimes straddle the fault line of fact and fiction.-x-Nivedita: a Hindustani Classical vocalist with the World Music album, Autumn Ballet, from Saregama India (erstwhile HMV). A performer and teacher, Nivedita had led a one-hour TV program dedicated to Major Sandeep Unnikrishnan, martyr of Mumbai 26/11, where she sang Vande Mataram in the oldest known tune.Ashis: a software entrepreneur, a raconteur who has anchored major musical shows, a writer in mainstream newspapers and inflight magazines on travel, music and culture, and author of the book – Gandhi and the London Cabby. From Rumi to Bankim Chandra, Ashis has written about in major newspapers.Nivedita and Ashis had created a vote song, “Chalo Vote de” which was adopted by the Election Commission of India. Their song was later expanded in ten Indian languages by Janagraha.

name: kowdhiworkshop
description: A two - day workshop ( 28th - 29th March 2026 )Kowdhi is a traditional hand-quilting technique from Uttara Kannada region, passed down through generations of women. A Kowdhi quilt is traditionally made using seven layers of old, used fabric—often sarees, dupattas, and other scraps—stitched together to form the base. On top of this layered base, colourful fabric scraps are hand-stitched in concentric patterns—starting from the outer edges and moving inward toward the centre—a rhythmical process that gives the quilt both its structure and its vibrant appearance. The process is deeply rooted in storytelling, sustainability, and community rituals.Kowdhi making becomes a meeting place for women after a long day's labor. It becomes a space for conversation, friendship, appreciating each other, and exchanging skills. Kowdhis are also frozen stories of birth, death, geography, and the landscape of the artisans immediate surroundings. This is what makes the Kowdhi extraordinary.BuDa folklore is building a learning community among the urban people and bringing awareness of this traditional hand-stitching techniques for the urban participants through workshops.About the Workshop:We invite you to something slow, beautiful, and deeply rooted — a two full days traditional Kowdhi making workshop with master artisans from the BuDa Folklore family. You will be guided by master artisans on how to join pieces of cloth — using just needle, thread, and scrap fabric. You will get a chance to listen to the stories of the Kowdhi and the connection between motifs, landscape and cultural memory. One workshop can hold space between 12 to 15 members.Open to adults of all ages, Gender no Bar.Workshop is open to a novice or an expert, in quilt making. You will enjoy spending timewith our artisans and making a mini-quilt of your own.To prepare for the workshop, please bring:1.⁠ ⁠Soft, old dupatta/sari/muslin (later cut into 12" squares) for the base and inner layers2.⁠ ⁠Cotton Scrap fabric – vibrant pieces (plain or printed), to be joined together on top of the base 3.⁠ ⁠Old saree/dupatta border OR 1.5 yards of printed fabric/lace – for a 2" border4.⁠ ⁠Scissors5.⁠ ⁠Pins (optional)We will provide thread and needles.Please note:1. You can donate old cotton or cotton-silk sarees, cotton fabrics, or dupattas (damaged or unused). Through the Kowdhi Collective, these textiles are thoughtfully recycled and upcycled into something new.2. Lunch cost not included.3. There will be an exhibition and sale from BuDa folklore.Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/kowdhicollective/Age 18+

name: My Home is Dissent: Book Launch & Poetry Workshop
description: My Home is Dissent is a luminous poetry collection by Pooja Ugrani that constructs verse as one might build a home—brick by tender brick, with the mortar of memory, desire, and defiance. Published by Poetrywala, an imprint of Paperwall Publishing, this collection moves fluidly between the everyday and the elemental, where the domestic becomes a site of quiet resistance and reimagining. Ugrani's dissent hums through acts of nurture and refusal, through the courage to remain soft in a world that demands armour. Her poems measure the dimensions of love, labour, and loss through the delicate instruments of memory, architecture, and motherhood. Formally supple and sensorial, cerebral and sensuous, her work folds the architectural and the emotional into one continuum. This world of words writes of labour, love, and dissent as acts of design—as deliberate gestures that hold the self together. Praised by acclaimed authors including Sampurna Chattarji, Maithreyi Karnoor, and poet Srividya Sivakumar, this collection participates physically in the making of poetry, not only through language but also through the palpability of the body's experiences. Here is a poet testing her wings, where dissension may well be a heartbeat away from dissection. It is currently out on Amazon for sale: https://amzn.in/d/0gY3DXTfAbout the Poet: Pooja Ugrani, architect, teacher and mother, lives her life in two cities, Bombay and Bangalore.Her poems are included in the anthologies The Shape of a Poem - The Red River Book of Contemporary Erotic Poetry, The Kali project anthology - Invoking the Goddess within/ Indian Women's Voices by Indie Blue Publishing LLC, the Yearbook of Indian Poetry 2022 and Po'try, an anthology of Bangalore poets. Her poem, Gundalamma Palem was shortlisted as one of the forty poems out of over thousand international entries for the Alpine Fellowship 2023. Her poetry has been published in The Punch Magazine, Cafe Dissensus Everyday, Mom Egg Review (New York), Hākārā, Voice and Verse Poetry Magazine (Hong Kong), The Tiger Moth Review (Singapore), The Alipore Post, The Woman Inc - TWI Poetry, and the RIC Journal (Jaipur-Paris). Ugrani was invited to read at the Bangalore Poetry Festival and by the Champaca bookstore at the Bangalore International Center on the occasion of International Womens' Day. She was a featured poet at Tuesdays with the Bard at Urban Solace, Ulsoor, in December 2019. While she is relaxing, Pooja Ugrani explores origami with children and adults, sings Bollywood songs and dreams of a trek in the Himalayas.Age: 15 and above

name: SAHA - A Woman's Day Special
description: There are seasons where you are strong for everyone else.Where you show up, deliver, hold, manage, organise, respond.And then there are moments when you quietly wonderWhen was the last time I chose myself?This Women’s Day, gift yourself or the special women in your life a chance to be part of a transformative workshop to step out of performance and into presence.Many women today are:•⁠ ⁠Mentally overstimulated•⁠ ⁠Constantly on edge•⁠ ⁠Clear about responsibilities but unclear about personal desiresSAHA is designed to gently interrupt that pattern.This experience is for you if:•⁠ ⁠You are successful but tired•⁠ ⁠You are capable but craving direction•⁠ ⁠You prioritise everyone else and are ready to prioritise yourselfCome experience mat pilates with Akshata the First and only Qualified Fletcher Pilates teacher from India. She believes Pilates helps not just build strength but also in understanding our body better which helping us be kind to ourselves through awareness of movementEnjoy the food intentionally prepared by Varsha a chef, nutritionist, and the founder of SORU. Her work celebrates the diversity and quiet wisdom of traditional Tamil cooking, reimagined for today without losing its soul.Set intentional goals with the therapists from Utsmaya, a therapeutic space founded by Mamtha Rajesh, a senior therapist with 26 years of experience, alongside co-founder Mihika Rajesh and psychotherapist Muskan Siyal. The team offers therapy, breathwork, workshops, and retreats focused on deep trauma healing, nervous system regulation, and intentional personal growth.

name: Think and Build Hours - Bangalore
description: Session 1 - Founding Room. Think & Build Hours is a small, curated working session for people who enjoy solving meaningful problems with other sharp minds.Without hierarchy. Without escalation paths. It’s about building something together and discovering who you think well with. Trust us your brain can do wonders when titles disappear. You’ll get three hours with passionate co-workers you didn’t know you needed. And somewhere between, something valuable emerges. Of course, we keep it fun and cozy - like those rooms you see in cool documentaries. Thoughtful people, open conversations, ideas on whiteboards, coffee / tender coconut on the side. (Summer of '26 you know!)In the room:A product thinker structures the ambiguity.A marketer reframes the narrative.A founder questions the viability.A designer visualises the leap.Every session centers around a real problem from tech, product, brand, or business - the kind that brings teams together with in the real world. Together, you’ll break it down, debate approaches, sketch directions, and build clarity under constraint.What you’ll gain:• Meet people by actually working with them for three hours, not just exchanging introductions.• Experience how others think, structure ambiguity, and make decisions.• Sharpen your ability to articulate ideas clearly.• Build faster alignment in unfamiliar teams.• Leave with sharper thinking and connections that feel earned.Come experience what it feels like to operate like a founding team - without hierarchy and ladders.The room is intentionally small. If you value thoughtful conversations, honest debate, and real collaboration - you’ll feel at home here.

name: Oota & Aata Edition 4
description: 'Travel back in time to peaceful simple evenings with friends & family' Oota & Aata is an intimate, hyper-local event series that invites people to pause, gather, and play like we used to — back in the day.A curated experience set in Sabha inspired by balmy Bangalore’s late afternoons, games your ajji loved paired with comforting tea-time snacks inviting a slower kind of joy.Oota & Aata Edition 4Edition 4 comes to you presented by Anand Sweets, Bangalore's beloved name in traditional mithai and savouries, bringing their signature warmth and authenticity to an evening that's about going back to our roots. Expect their touch woven into the flavours of the evening.Recreate the memory of a childhood home where slow weekend evenings were spent in playful exploration with loved ones, as you choose from one of the games like Alaguli Mane/Pallanguzhi, Pagade, Aadu Huli/ Tiger & Goat to explore.This time, Oota & Aata will take place over 3 sessions — morning (10:30am–1pm), afternoon (1:30pm–4pm), and evening (4:30–7pm). Please choose your slot accordingly. Alternatively, there are limited DAY PASSES for guests who wish to stay on or play for longer than 2.5hours. The event will also feature a snack edit by Anand Sweets — a thoughtful mix of their signature sweets and savouries to keep you fuelled through every move and counter-move. Paired with ambient music, it's the kind of evening that feels just right. Gather your game-night crew, a friend or come solo for an evening of playful (could get competitive) fun and discovery!About Urbanaut ProductionsUrbanaut Productions curates events and experiences that sit at the intersection of art, culture, food, history, and drink. Our concept are designed to pull you out of the everyday and into something worth remembering.About Roll The DiceRoll The Dice is dedicated to reinventing the experience of playing traditional Indian board games. These games are most enjoyable and beneficial when played by people across generations, offering memory boosts, sharper thinking, relaxation, and improved cognitive skills.About SabhaSabha is imagined as a cultural space housed in a meticulously restored 160-year-old CVS Sabha school, part of the RBANM's Educational Charities legacy. Envisioned as a hub for the arts, ideas, and community, Sabha is a place where heritage meets contemporary expression.Please note - Event is only for ages 5+. Ticket is mandatory for all ages.

name: tunturu’s supper club
description: What happens when the food you grew up with steps into a modern Indian fine dine experience?Tunturu invites you to an intimate, community-style supper club centred around all things delicate and all things food. Chef Ritika Deshpande is the in-house chef, bringing her learnings from the kitchens she has worked in into everything they create. Here, food is explored through memory, process, and play. Tunturu creates spaces for people to gather and connect through supper clubs, grazing tables, curated plates, bakes, and hands-on experiences.

name: Shutter Island Screening
description: Shutter Island is a film about the fragile architecture of the mind, and the stories we tell ourselves to survive.On the surface, it unfolds like an investigation, a disappearance, a remote island, a search for truth. But beneath that lies something far more unsettling: grief, guilt, and the human ability to construct realities that protect us from what we cannot bear to face. It’s not just a thriller. It’s a meditation on memory, trauma, and the thin line between perception and denial. The film asks a question that lingers long after the credits roll:What if the truth is not something you discover, but something you resist?An atmospheric screening, followed by an open conversation not just about what happened, but about what it means.
